title: ILI9341 2.4-inch TFT SPI Colour Display
design_question: How to add a compact full-colour graphical display to an Arduino or ESP32 for waveform visualisation or instrumentation panels?
selected_approach: 2.4-inch ILI9341 TFT breakout (240×320, SPI, integrated XPT2046 touch). Wired to SPI bus: CS=D10, DC=D9, RST=D8, MOSI=D11, SCK=D13 (Arduino); or SPI VSPI (ESP32). TFT_eSPI library with SPI clock 40 MHz (ESP32) or 8 MHz (Arduino Uno). Backlight on PWM for brightness control. Drawing partial-screen 240-pixel-wide waveform lines at 60 fps using TFT_eSPI's fast horizontal line functions.
rationale: ILI9341 provides 65K colour at a well-supported price point (< £4). TFT_eSPI supports ESP32 and AVR with DMA on ESP32, enabling 60 fps partial-screen updates. XPT2046 touch gives menu navigation without extra hardware. 240×320 resolution adequate for waveforms, menus, and gauges.

alternatives.reason_rejected: OLED is monochrome and smaller — adequate for text/simple icons but insufficient for colour waveform display or multi-element instrumentation panels.
ST7789 supports faster SPI (80 MHz on ESP32) and has no CS pin simplifying wiring, but 240×240 aspect ratio less suitable for landscape waveform display.

verification_notes: FACT: 'ILI9341 2.4-inch TFT LCD — SPI Display Interface' confirms RGB565 format, SPI timing, XPT2046 touch, and partial-screen update strategy.
supporting_fact_titles: ILI9341 2.4-inch TFT LCD — SPI Display Interface
